  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c1", "name": "Truth-seeking", "aliases": ["Truth-seeking principle"], "description": "Unbiased AI Principle requiring LLMs to be truthful on factual prompts, prioritizing historical accuracy, scientific inquiry, and objectivity, and acknowledging uncertainty.", "grounding_span": "(1) Truth-seeking", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "c2", "name": "Ideological Neutrality", "aliases": ["Ideological neutrality principle"], "description": "Unbiased AI Principle requiring LLMs to be neutral, nonpartisan tools that do not manipulate responses in favor of ideological dogmas.", "grounding_span": "(2) Ideological Neutrality", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "c3", "name": "Large Language Model", "aliases": ["LLM", "LLMs"], "description": "Generative AI model trained on vast, diverse datasets that generates natural-language responses to user prompts; the primary subject of the memorandum's procurement requirements.", "grounding_span": "Large Language Models (LLMs) procured", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "c4", "name": "Trustworthy artificial intelligence", "aliases": ["trustworthy AI"], "description": "Policy goal of promoting innovation and use of trustworthy AI, which the Unbiased AI Principles serve.", "grounding_span": "promoting innovation and use of trustworthy artificial", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "c5", "name": "Harmful ideological bias in AI outputs", "aliases": ["ideological bias", "harmful bias", "social agendas"], "description": "The harm the memorandum seeks to prevent: LLM outputs shaped by harmful ideological biases or social agendas.", "grounding_span": "free from harmful ideological biases or", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "c6", "name": "Federal procurement of LLMs", "aliases": ["LLM procurement", "procuring an LLM"], "description": "Agency acquisition of LLMs, the setting in which agencies must obtain vendor information sufficient to assess compliance with the Unbiased AI Principles.", "grounding_span": "When procuring an LLM, agencies must obtain sufficient information from the vendor", "location": "Appendix A §1, page 1"},
    {"id": "c7", "name": "Contractual requirements addressing compliance", "aliases": ["contractual requirements"], "description": "Requirements agencies must include in solicitations/orders addressing LLM compliance with the Unbiased AI Principles.", "grounding_span": "1. CONTRACTUAL REQUIREMENTS", "location": "Appendix A §1, page 1"},
    {"id": "c8", "name": "Acceptable Use Policy", "aliases": ["AUP"], "description": "Developer-drafted document characterizing appropriate and inappropriate use of an LLM product; required at minimum transparency threshold.", "grounding_span": "i. Acceptable Use Policy.", "location": "Appendix A §1.A, page 2"},
    {"id": "c9", "name": "Model, System, and Data Cards", "aliases": ["model cards", "system cards", "data cards"], "description": "Developer materials outlining essential information about the model, system, and/or data, including training summaries, risks, mitigations, and benchmark scores.", "grounding_span": "ii. Model, System, and/or Data Cards.", "location": "Appendix A §1.A, page 2"},
    {"id": "c10", "name": "End User Resources", "aliases": [], "description": "Product tutorials, developer guides, or other tools helping customers ensure proper LLM use.", "grounding_span": "iii. End User Resources.", "location": "Appendix A §1.A, page 2"},
    {"id": "c11", "name": "Mechanism for End User Feedback", "aliases": ["end user feedback mechanism"], "description": "A general inbox, product point of contact, or similar mechanism for reporting outputs that violate the Unbiased AI Principles.", "grounding_span": "iv. Mechanism For End User Feedback.", "location": "Appendix A §1.A, page 2"},
    {"id": "c12", "name": "Minimum Threshold for LLM Transparency", "aliases": ["minimum transparency threshold"], "description": "Baseline set of vendor documentation agencies must request in LLM solicitations.", "grounding_span": "A. Minimum Threshold for LLM Transparency", "location": "Appendix A §1.A, page 2"},
    {"id": "c13", "name": "Enhanced LLM Transparency", "aliases": ["enhanced transparency threshold"], "description": "Additional vendor information an agency may request when it needs more assurance of alignment with the Principles.", "grounding_span": "B. Threshold for Enhanced LLM Transparency", "location": "Appendix A §1.B, page 2"},
    {"id": "c14", "name": "Pre-training and post-training activities", "aliases": ["pre-training", "post-training"], "description": "Actions impacting factuality and grounding of outputs, including system-level prompts, content moderation, red teaming, and foreign development disclosures.", "grounding_span": "i. Pre-Training and Post-Training Activities.", "location": "Appendix A §1.B, page 2"},
    {"id": "c15", "name": "System-level prompts", "aliases": ["system prompts"], "description": "Natural-language instructions to the model specifying guidelines on responding to user queries, especially where information is incomplete or contradictory.", "grounding_span": "System-level prompts that provide natural language instructions to the", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.i, page 2"},
    {"id": "c16", "name": "Content moderation and safety filters", "aliases": ["safety filters", "content moderation"], "description": "Controls restricting the type of outputs a model produces.", "grounding_span": "The type of outputs restricted via content moderation and safety filters.", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.i, page 3"},
    {"id": "c17", "name": "Red teaming", "aliases": [], "description": "Continuous assessment of a model to protect against incidents of bias in generated output.", "grounding_span": "Use of red teaming as a means of continuously assessing the model to", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.i, page 3"},
    {"id": "c18", "name": "Bias evaluations", "aliases": [], "description": "Vendor-conducted evaluations of model bias and the methodology used, e.g., testing prompt pairs for politically-oriented topics.", "grounding_span": "Results of bias evaluations conducted by the vendor and the methodology", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.ii, page 3"},
    {"id": "c19", "name": "Model evaluations", "aliases": [], "description": "Category of enhanced-transparency information covering bias evaluations and benchmark scores for bias, helpfulness, honesty, and accuracy.", "grounding_span": "ii. Model Evaluations.", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.ii, page 3"},
    {"id": "c20", "name": "Benchmark scores", "aliases": ["LLM benchmarks", "benchmarks"], "description": "Vendor benchmark scores measuring a model's bias, helpfulness, honesty, or accuracy, including cross-language comparisons where necessary.", "grounding_span": "Benchmark scores for vendor evaluations to measure a model’s bias,", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.ii, page 3"},
    {"id": "c21", "name": "Enterprise-level controls", "aliases": ["enterprise controls", "governance tools"], "description": "Governance tools such as customizable system instructions, evaluation tools, and source-citation features that provide visibility into output provenance.", "grounding_span": "iii. Enterprise-Level Controls.", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.iii, page 3"},
    {"id": "c22", "name": "Third party modifications", "aliases": ["third-party modifications"], "description": "Additional controls (classifiers, system prompts, fine-tuning, content filters) applied by a vendor that is not the direct LLM developer.", "grounding_span": "iv. Third Party Modifications.", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.iv, page 3"},
    {"id": "c23", "name": "Materiality requirement", "aliases": ["materiality"], "description": "Explicit identification of transparency requirements as material to eligibility and payment, supporting termination for default upon uncorrected noncompliance.", "grounding_span": "C. Materiality Requirement", "location": "Appendix A §1.C, page 3"},
    {"id": "c24", "name": "Applicability factors", "aliases": [], "description": "Factors an agency considers in deciding whether to apply the memorandum's requirements to agency-developed LLMs or to non-LLM AI models.", "grounding_span": "2. APPLICABILITY FACTORS", "location": "Appendix A §2, page 3"},
    {"id": "c25", "name": "National security systems", "aliases": ["NSS"], "description": "Systems defined in 44 U.S.C. 3552(b)(6) that the memorandum does not apply to, though application to them is encouraged where practicable.", "grounding_span": "They do not apply to national security systems, as defined", "location": "Scope, page 2"},
    {"id": "c26", "name": "Human accountability for agency AI decisions", "aliases": ["human accountability"], "description": "The goal that humans remain accountable for agency actions and decisions involving LLMs.", "grounding_span": "agencies will ensure that humans are accountable for", "location": "Appendix A, page 1"},
    {"id": "c27", "name": "Small language model", "aliases": ["SLM"], "description": "A smaller language model that an agency may develop, to which analogous documentation expectations apply.", "grounding_span": "an agency is developing an LLM or small language model,", "location": "Appendix A §2.A, page 4"},
    {"id": "c28", "name": "Generative AI capabilities", "aliases": ["image, voice, or multimodal generation"], "description": "Non-LLM generative AI tools (image, voice, multimodal) for which the guidance should inform documentation requirements where practicable.", "grounding_span": "other types of generative AI capabilities, such as tools", "location": "Appendix A §2.B, page 4"},
    {"id": "c29", "name": "Provenance of model outputs", "aliases": ["source citation", "output provenance"], "description": "Product features requiring a model to cite sources or otherwise provide visibility into the provenance of its outputs.", "grounding_span": "otherwise provide visibility into the provenance of the model’s outputs.", "location": "Appendix A §1.B.iii, page 3"},
    {"id": "c30", "name": "Vendor risk management", "aliases": ["risk management actions"], "description": "A vendor's risk management actions at the model, system, and/or application level used to establish compliance with the Principles.", "grounding_span": "assess a vendor’s risk management actions at the model,", "location": "Appendix A §1, page 1"},
    {"id": "c31", "name": "Software supply chain / third-party LLM provider", "aliases": ["resellers", "intermediary deployers", "software integrators"], "description": "The chain of resellers, integrators, and platform operators through which agencies indirectly obtain LLMs, affecting information availability.", "grounding_span": "depending on the vendor’s role within the software supply chain", "location": "Appendix A §1, page 1"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d1", "term": "LLM", "verbatim_text": "a large language model, which is a generative AI model trained on vast, diverse datasets that enable the model to generate natural-language responses to user prompts.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-07-23", "grounding_span": "a large language model, which is a generative AI model trained on", "location": "footnote 3, page 1"},
    {"id": "d2", "term": "agency", "verbatim_text": "\"agency\" means an executive department, military department, or any independent establishment within the meaning of 5 U.S.C. 101, 102, and 104(1), respectively, or any wholly owned Government corporation within the meaning of 31 U.S.C. 9101.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-12-11", "grounding_span": "executive department, military department, or any independent establishment within the meaning", "location": "Scope, page 2"},
    {"id": "d3", "term": "Truth-seeking", "verbatim_text": "LLMs shall be truthful in responding to user prompts seeking factual information or analysis. LLMs shall prioritize historical accuracy, scientific inquiry, and objectivity, and shall acknowledge uncertainty where reliable information is incomplete or contradictory.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-07-23", "grounding_span": "shall be truthful in responding to user prompts seeking factual information", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "d4", "term": "Ideological Neutrality", "verbatim_text": "LLMs shall be neutral, nonpartisan tools that do not manipulate responses in favor of ideological dogmas. Developers shall not intentionally encode partisan or ideological judgments into an LLM's outputs unless those judgments are prompted by or otherwise readily accessible to the end user.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2025-07-23", "grounding_span": "LLMs shall be neutral, nonpartisan tools that do not manipulate responses in favor", "location": "Overview, page 1"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "On July 23, 2025, President Trump signed Executive Order 14319, Preventing Woke AI in the Federal Government.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "On July 23, 2025, President Trump signed Executive Order 14319, Preventing Woke Al", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "The E.O. identifies two Unbiased AI Principles.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the E.O. identifies two Unbiased AI Principles", "location": "Overview, page 1"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "It is uncommon for a vendor to produce all three types (model, system, data) of summary cards.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "for a vendor to produce all three types (model, system, data) of summary cards.", "location": "Appendix A §1.A, page 2"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "In Federal procurement, it is common for vendors to provide agencies with access to LLMs and other AI indirectly, through resellers or other intermediary deployers of a developer's product.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "it is common for vendors to provide agencies with access to", "location": "Appendix A §1, page 1"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "This memorandum shall cease to have any force or effect two years after the date of its issuance, unless the Director of OMB provides otherwise.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "This memorandum shall cease to have any force or effect two years after the date of its", "location": "Agency Actions d, page 3"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "The Unbiased AI Principle of truth-seeking will likely be more relevant than ideological neutrality for LLMs that summarize cybersecurity incidents for a security operations center.", "claim_type": "speculative", "grounding_span": "the Unbiased AI Principle of “truth-seeking” will likely be more relevant than “ideological", "location": "footnote 14, page 2"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "No later than March 11, 2026, agencies must update their policies and procedures to ensure LLM procurement contracts include requirements addressing compliance with the Unbiased AI Principles.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "No later than March 11, 2026, agencies must update their policies and procedures", "location": "Agency Actions c, page 3"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "By complying with the directives, policies, and this memorandum, agencies will ensure that humans are accountable for agency actions and decisions involving LLMs.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "agencies will ensure that humans are accountable for", "location": "Appendix A, page 1"}
  ],
